reason: shell.exec is unconditionally forbidden at priority 100 and cannot be overridden by any lower-priority permit. Replace shell.exec usage with fine-grained tools: filesystem.read, filesystem.write, filesystem.list, filesystem.delete, web.search, web.fetch, or web.post.

unsafe_admin_exec is a legacy privileged-execution wrapper whose action_class is shell.exec — already unconditionally forbidden at priority 100. This tool-level entry makes the restriction explicit and prevents accidental permit rules from lower-priority entries. Operators who need limited privileged execution must configure explicit HITL approval and restrict commands to a documented allowlist.

Enumerating credential keys exposes the full secret namespace and is treated with the same HITL-gated caution as credential.read, credential.write, and credential.rotate. Operators who need automated listing may lower the priority or narrow the scope using allowed_key_prefixes.

permissions.elevate (sudo / su / doas / passwd) is forbidden by default in v1.3.2. Privilege elevation has unbounded blast radius once root is granted; the v1.3.2 typed-tool roadmap (docs/release-plans/v1.3.2.md §2.2) deliberately ships NO typed wrapper for these binaries. Operators who need privilege escalation must either lower the priority of this rule for their environment, or use unsafe_admin_exec under the documented escape-hatch protocol (CLAWTHORITY_ENABLE_UNSAFE_ADMIN_EXEC + per-call HITL + audit log).

VCS remote operations (git clone, git push) communicate with upstream repositories and carry network-side effects. Permitted by default; operators should restrict to internal or allowlisted remote URLs in high-security environments.

Outbound email is permitted by default. Operators SHOULD restrict to an approved sender domain and recipient list to prevent data exfiltration. Set allowed_recipient_domains to your organisation's domains and allowed_recipients to a fixed list for tighter control.

Package installation (e.g. npm install) modifies the dependency tree and may fetch code from external registries. Permitted by default for development environments. In production or locked environments, consider restricting to a private registry or setting effect to forbid.

HTTP mutation methods (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) send data to external services. Permitted by default. Operators should restrict to an approved URL allowlist using allowed_url_patterns to prevent data leakage to arbitrary endpoints.

Outbound webhook calls deliver payloads to external HTTP endpoints. Permitted by default. Operators should restrict to approved webhook URLs using allowed_url_patterns to prevent exfiltration or SSRF.
Slack messaging is permitted by default. Operators should restrict to approved channels using allowed_channels to limit blast radius and prevent data leakage across workspace channels.
VCS write operations (git add, branch, checkout, commit, merge, reset) modify local repository state. Permitted by default. Remote-side effects are governed separately by the vcs.remote policy.
Filesystem write operations are permitted by default. Operators should restrict to project workspace paths using allowed_paths to prevent writes to sensitive system locations.
HTTP GET and HEAD fetches are permitted by default. Operators should restrict to HTTPS-only and allowlisted domains using allowed_url_patterns to prevent SSRF against internal services.
Browser-based page scraping is permitted by default. Operators should restrict to HTTPS targets and allowlisted domains using allowed_url_patterns.
Compilation tasks (e.g. npm run build) are permitted by default for development and CI environments. Operators can restrict to allowlisted build scripts if needed.
Package script execution (e.g. npm run <script>) is permitted by default. Operators should review the set of permitted script names in high-security environments.
Archive creation compresses files into a single artifact. Permitted by default. Operators should restrict source paths using allowed_paths to prevent bundling of sensitive files.

Archive extraction writes files to the target directory. Permitted by default. Operators should restrict the destination path using allowed_paths to prevent path-traversal writes outside the workspace.
Web search is read-only and carries no mutation risk. Permitted by default for all agents.
Running test suites is a safe, read-oriented operation that does not mutate external state. Permitted by default.
VCS read operations (git diff, log, status) are non-mutating and safe by default.
Directory listing is a read-only operation and is permitted by default. Operators may restrict to specific paths using allowed_paths if directory enumeration of sensitive areas is a concern.

System information reads (environment variables, system metadata) are non-mutating. Permitted by default. Note: environment variables may contain secrets — operators with strict secret hygiene should consider a narrower allowed_env_vars list.
Package enumeration (e.g. pip list, npm list) is a read-only introspection operation with no external side effects.
Linting is a read-only static-analysis pass with no external side effects. Permitted for all agents.
Archive listing inspects an archive's table of contents without extracting files. Safe and read-only.
